description_long: This object is a vintage-style floatplane with a single set of straight, high-mounted wings and two large pontoons for water landings. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which features a conventional tail design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two seats. The engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller, indicating a piston engine configuration. The main color scheme consists of muted maroon and gray tones, with the pontoons and some structural elements in darker shades. The overall design suggests a classic seaplane suitable for both water and land-based operations.
description: A vintage floatplane with high-mounted straight wings, twin pontoons, and a nose-mounted propeller engine in maroon and gray colors.
description_view_0: Side-rear view showing the high-mounted wing, twin pontoons, and the streamlined tail with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Direct rear view highlighting the full wingspan, twin pontoons, and the tail assembly.
description_view_2: Side-front view displaying the nose-mounted propeller, cockpit placement, and the float landing gear.
description_view_3: Direct front view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit, and the symmetrical arrangement of the pontoons and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Twin float landing gear, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit likely configured for one or two seats with a clear canopy.
